pages.full_text:
sunny. We have camped at [?Port mirail] in the municipal park. Near the sports field. V. few people about. The [?Accent] [?acceuil] woman gave us the name of a local restaurant that had closed 3 years previously. A nice has-been town. We ended up having a good meal at le Grand Condé. The camp site only charges [strikethrough]£[/strikethrough] € 5.5. We just missed a huge rainshower. At supper we sat next to a man [?mending] leather machines - Louis Vuitton
__Thursday__ Royal wedding for Prince Harry which we are going to miss. (Here I missed out that we camped nr. [?Chinee] country)
We went the wrong way north & ended up driving on a beautiful road, past [?Bonneil] village. I'd like to go back & walk there, near Châton-Thierry.
On to a large Auchan. Simon had trouble getting more camping gas because of a stupid cashier. However we reached an earlier Channel Train with time to spare. Men were using a drone in different places. [?Walking] something. What? Why? A man stood with cones in a circle round him on different sites.
    We reached Roksell Farm
& William joined us for lunch. We spent a
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence, continuing from a previous page. 'Port mirail' uncertain — could be 'Port Mirail' or another spelling. 'Bonneil' could be 'Bonneuil' or similar French village near Château-Thierry. 'Châton-Thierry' likely = Château-Thierry. 'Roksell Farm' may be 'Roksell' or similar Kent hop farm — context of hop picking follows on right page. Reference to 'Royal wedding for Prince Harry' anchors this to May 2018 (Harry & Meghan wed 19 May 2018). Parenthetical annotation appears to be a later addition noting they camped near '[?Chinee] country'.

pages.full_text:
couple of hours looking at [?stuff] hop picking machines. We are buying stuff including a plucker bank for £10,000. A nice man, Anthony, 28 years old. Very self-assured. His first cousin has inherited [strikethrough]the[/strikethrough] half the grand-father's land (originally 3 or 400 acres) but doesn't work as hard. Anthony grows other things besides hops, including onions. He has an amazing new Wolf (double wolf machine) & was taking the Germans to the airport.
    We decided to come back to the Plough campsite again in Stalisfield. — A good evening. Another good supper.
__Friday__ Sunshine flooded the camping van at breakfast. Off to Canterbury. 597 Augustine arrived. His tome was here for a while. 1170 Thomas à Becket murdered. Lovely window of Adam Delving. We liked the undercroft. Gormley [?floats] body sculpture of nails. Great Norman pillars & capitols. 2 columns from 601. Chapter house with good ceiling & 1896 windows.
    People were friendly & bus driver told us a better place for the van Park & Ride.
pages.notes: Right page continues from left. 'Roksell Farm' on left page appears to be a hop farm — context here confirms hop picking machinery visit. 'Plough campsite in Stalisfield' = The Plough pub campsite in Stalisfield, Kent. Canterbury Cathedral visit described: references to Augustine (597), Thomas à Becket (murdered 1170), Adam Delving window, Gormley nail sculpture, undercroft. 'Gormley floats' uncertain — could be 'floats' or another word describing the sculpture display. Year 2018 supported by Prince Harry's royal wedding reference on left page.
